title: Assertion arguments should be passed in the correct order
description: Assertions are statements that check whether certain conditions are true. They are used to validate that the actual results of a code snippet match the expected outcomes. By using assertions, developers can ensure that their code behaves as intended and identify potential bugs or issues early in the development process.
recommendation: Ensure assertion arguments are in correct order: actual value first, expected value second.

noncompliant: expect(expected, actual).toBe(); // Wrong order - expected first

checks.message: Ensure assertion arguments are in correct order: actual value first, expected value second.
